000046  1ba8              SUBS     r0,r5,r6
000048  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
00004a  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
                  |L36.78|
;;;1274               }
;;;1275               else
;;;1276               {
;;;1277                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1278               }
;;;1279           }
;;;1280           xAlreadyYielded = xTaskResumeAll();
00004e  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskResumeAll
000052  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;1281   
;;;1282           /* Force a reschedule if xTaskResumeAll has not already done so, we may
;;;1283            * have put ourselves to sleep. */
;;;1284           if( xAlreadyYielded == pdFALSE )
000054  b93f              CBNZ     r7,|L36.102|
;;;1285           {
;;;1286               portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000056  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00005a  4905              LDR      r1,|L36.112|
00005c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00005e  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000062  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L36.102|
;;;1287           }
;;;1288           else
;;;1289           {
;;;1290               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1291           }
;;;1292   
;;;1293           return xShouldDelay;
000066  4648              MOV      r0,r9
;;;1294       }
000068  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;1295   
                          ENDP

                  |L36.108|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L36.112|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotify||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotify PROC
;;;4848   
;;;4849       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotify( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d5ff0          PUSH     {r4-r12,lr}
;;;4850                                      UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;4851                                      uint32_t ulValue,
;;;4852                                      eNotifyAction eAction,
;;;4853                                      uint32_t * pulPreviousNotificationValue )
;;;4854       {
000004  4681              MOV      r9,r0
000006  460d              MOV      r5,r1
000008  4616              MOV      r6,r2
00000a  461f              MOV      r7,r3
00000c  f8dda028          LDR      r10,[sp,#0x28]
;;;4855           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;4856           BaseType_t xReturn = pdPASS;
000010  f04f0b01          MOV      r11,#1
;;;4857           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;4858   
;;;4859           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4860           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;4861           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
000014  464c              MOV      r4,r9
;;;4862   
;;;4863           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000016  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4864           {
;;;4865               if( pulPreviousNotificationValue != NULL )
00001a  f1ba0f00          CMP      r10,#0
00001e  d005              BEQ      |L37.44|
;;;4866               {
;;;4867                   *pulPreviousNotificationValue = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000020  f1040044          ADD      r0,r4,#0x44
000024  f8500025          LDR      r0,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
000028  f8ca0000          STR      r0,[r10,#0]
                  |L37.44|
;;;4868               }
;;;4869   
;;;4870               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
00002c  f1040048          ADD      r0,r4,#0x48
000030  f8108005          LDRB     r8,[r0,r5]
;;;4871   
;;;4872               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
000034  2102              MOVS     r1,#2
000036  5541              STRB     r1,[r0,r5]
;;;4873   
;;;4874               switch( eAction )
000038  2f05              CMP      r7,#5
00003a  d229              BCS      |L37.144|
00003c  e8dff007          TBB      [pc,r7]
000040  27030d17          DCB      0x27,0x03,0x0d,0x17
000044  1c00              DCB      0x1c,0x00
;;;4875               {
;;;4876                   case eSetBits:
;;;4877                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] |= ulValue;
000046  f1040044          ADD      r0,r4,#0x44
00004a  f8500025          LDR      r0,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
00004e  4330              ORRS     r0,r0,r6
000050  f1040144          ADD      r1,r4,#0x44
000054  f8410025          STR      r0,[r1,r5,LSL #2]
;;;4878                       break;
000058  e01b              B        |L37.146|
;;;4879   
;;;4880                   case eIncrement:
;;;4881                       ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
00005a  f1040044          ADD      r0,r4,#0x44
00005e  f8500025          LDR      r0,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
000062  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000064  f1040144          ADD      r1,r4,#0x44
000068  f8410025          STR      r0,[r1,r5,LSL #2]
;;;4882                       break;
00006c  e011              B        |L37.146|
;;;4883   
;;;4884                   case eSetValueWithOverwrite:
;;;4885                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
00006e  f1040044          ADD      r0,r4,#0x44
000072  f8406025          STR      r6,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
;;;4886                       break;
000076  e00c              B        |L37.146|
;;;4887   
;;;4888                   case eSetValueWithoutOverwrite:
;;;4889   
;;;4890                       if( ucOriginalNotifyState != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000078  f1b80f02          CMP      r8,#2
00007c  d004              BEQ      |L37.136|
;;;4891                       {
;;;4892                           pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
00007e  f1040044          ADD      r0,r4,#0x44
000082  f8406025          STR      r6,[r0,r5,LSL #2]
000086  e001              B        |L37.140|
                  |L37.136|
;;;4893                       }
;;;4894                       else
;;;4895                       {
;;;4896                           /* The value could not be written to the task. */
;;;4897                           xReturn = pdFAIL;
000088  f04f0b00          MOV      r11,#0
                  |L37.140|
;;;4898                       }
;;;4899   
;;;4900                       break;
00008c  e001              B        |L37.146|
;;;4901   
;;;4902                   case eNoAction:
;;;4903   
;;;4904                       /* The task is being notified without its notify value being
;;;4905                        * updated. */
;;;4906                       break;
00008e  e000              B        |L37.146|
                  |L37.144|
;;;4907   
;;;4908                   default:
;;;4909   
;;;4910                       /* Should not get here if all enums are handled.
;;;4911                        * Artificially force an assert by testing a value the
;;;4912                        * compiler can't assume is const. */
;;;4913                       configASSERT( xTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0 );
;;;4914   
;;;4915                       break;
000090  bf00              NOP      
                  |L37.146|
000092  bf00              NOP                            ;4878
;;;4916               }
;;;4917   
;;;4918               traceTASK_NOTIFY( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;4919   
;;;4920               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;4921                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;4922               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
000094  f1b80f01          CMP      r8,#1
000098  d14a              BNE      |L37.304|
;;;4923               {
;;;4924                   listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00009a  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
00009c  e9d41202          LDRD     r1,r2,[r4,#8]
0000a0  608a              STR      r2,[r1,#8]
0000a2  e9d42102          LDRD     r2,r1,[r4,#8]
0000a6  604a              STR      r2,[r1,#4]
0000a8  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
0000aa  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
0000ac  428a              CMP      r2,r1
0000ae  d101              BNE      |L37.180|
0000b0  68e1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0xc]
0000b2  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
                  |L37.180|
0000b4  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
0000b6  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
0000b8  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
0000ba  1e49              SUBS     r1,r1,#1
0000bc  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;4925                   prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
0000be  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000c2  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000c4  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
0000c6  491d              LDR      r1,|L37.316|
0000c8  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000ca  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
0000cc  491b              LDR      r1,|L37.316|
0000ce  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000d0  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000d2  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000d6  4a1a              LDR      r2,|L37.320|
0000d8  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000dc  6848              LDR      r0,[r1,#4]
0000de  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
0000e0  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
0000e2  60e2              STR      r2,[r4,#0xc]
0000e4  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
0000e6  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
0000e8  6051              STR      r1,[r2,#4]
0000ea  6081              STR      r1,[r0,#8]
0000ec  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000ee  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000f2  4a13              LDR      r2,|L37.320|
0000f4  eb020281          ADD      r2,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000f8  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
0000fa  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000fc  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000100  4a0f              LDR      r2,|L37.320|
000102  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
000106  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
000108  6ae2              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x2c]
00010a  eb020282          ADD      r2,r2,r2,LSL #2
00010e  4b0c              LDR      r3,|L37.320|
000110  f8431022          STR      r1,[r3,r2,LSL #2]
;;;4926   
;;;4927                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;4928                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;4929   
;;;4930                   #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;4931                   {
;;;4932                       /* If a task is blocked waiting for a notification then
;;;4933                        * xNextTaskUnblockTime might be set to the blocked task's time
;;;4934                        * out time.  If the task is unblocked for a reason other than
;;;4935                        * a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is normally left unchanged,
;;;4936                        * because it will automatically get reset to a new value when
;;;4937                        * the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;4938                        * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter
;;;4939                        * sleep mode at the earliest possible time - so reset
;;;4940                        * xNextTaskUnblockTime here to ensure it is updated at the
;;;4941                        * earliest possible time. */
;;;4942                       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;4943                   }
;;;4944                   #endif
;;;4945   
;;;4946                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000114  490b              LDR      r1,|L37.324|
000116  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000118  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00011a  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00011c  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00011e  d907              BLS      |L37.304|
;;;4947                   {
;;;4948                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;4949                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;4950                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
000120  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
000124  4908              LDR      r1,|L37.328|
000126  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000128  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00012c  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L37.304|
;;;4951                   }
;;;4952                   else
;;;4953                   {
;;;4954                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4955                   }
;;;4956               }
;;;4957               else
;;;4958               {
;;;4959                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4960               }
;;;4961           }
;;;4962           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000130  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;4963   
;;;4964           return xReturn;
000134  4658              MOV      r0,r11
;;;4965       }
000136  e8bd9ff0          POP      {r4-r12,pc}
;;;4966   
                          ENDP

00013a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L37.316|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L37.320|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L37.324|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L37.328|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR PROC
;;;4971   
;;;4972       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToNotify,
000000  e92d4ff0          PUSH     {r4-r11,lr}
;;;4973                                             UBaseType_t uxIndexToNotify,
;;;4974                                             uint32_t ulValue,
;;;4975                                             eNotifyAction eAction,
;;;4976                                             uint32_t * pulPreviousNotificationValue,
;;;4977                                             BaseType_t * pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken )
;;;4978       {
000004  4605              MOV      r5,r0
000006  460c              MOV      r4,r1
000008  e9dd6709          LDRD     r6,r7,[sp,#0x24]
;;;4979           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;4980           uint8_t ucOriginalNotifyState;
;;;4981           BaseType_t xReturn = pdPASS;
00000c  f04f0901          MOV      r9,#1
;;;4982           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;4983   
;;;4984           configASSERT( xTaskToNotify );
;;;4985           configASSERT( uxIndexToNotify < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4986   
;;;4987           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;4988            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;4989            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;4990            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;4991            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;4992            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;4993            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;4994            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;4995            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;4996            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;4997            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;4998            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;4999            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;5000            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;5001            * provided on the following link:
;;;5002            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;5003           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;5004   
;;;5005           pxTCB = xTaskToNotify;
000010  4629              MOV      r1,r5
;;;5006   
;;;5007           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
000012  bf00              NOP      
000014  f04f0abf          MOV      r10,#0xbf
000018  f3ef8011          MRS      r0,BASEPRI
00001c  f38a8811          MSR      BASEPRI,r10
000020  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000024  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
000028  bf00              NOP      
00002a  4680              MOV      r8,r0
;;;5008           {
;;;5009               if( pulPreviousNotificationValue != NULL )
00002c  b126              CBZ      r6,|L38.56|
;;;5010               {
;;;5011                   *pulPreviousNotificationValue = pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ];
00002e  f1010044          ADD      r0,r1,#0x44
000032  f8500024          LDR      r0,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
000036  6030              STR      r0,[r6,#0]
                  |L38.56|
;;;5012               }
;;;5013   
;;;5014               ucOriginalNotifyState = pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ];
000038  f1010048          ADD      r0,r1,#0x48
00003c  f810c004          LDRB     r12,[r0,r4]
;;;5015               pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToNotify ] = taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED;
000040  f04f0a02          MOV      r10,#2
000044  f800a004          STRB     r10,[r0,r4]
;;;5016   
;;;5017               switch( eAction )
000048  2b05              CMP      r3,#5
00004a  d229              BCS      |L38.160|
00004c  e8dff003          TBB      [pc,r3]
000050  27030d17          DCB      0x27,0x03,0x0d,0x17
000054  1c00              DCB      0x1c,0x00
;;;5018               {
;;;5019                   case eSetBits:
;;;5020                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] |= ulValue;
000056  f1010044          ADD      r0,r1,#0x44
00005a  f8500024          LDR      r0,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
00005e  4310              ORRS     r0,r0,r2
000060  f1010a44          ADD      r10,r1,#0x44
000064  f84a0024          STR      r0,[r10,r4,LSL #2]
;;;5021                       break;
000068  e01b              B        |L38.162|
;;;5022   
;;;5023                   case eIncrement:
;;;5024                       ( pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] )++;
00006a  f1010044          ADD      r0,r1,#0x44
00006e  f8500024          LDR      r0,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
000072  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000074  f1010a44          ADD      r10,r1,#0x44
000078  f84a0024          STR      r0,[r10,r4,LSL #2]
;;;5025                       break;
00007c  e011              B        |L38.162|
;;;5026   
;;;5027                   case eSetValueWithOverwrite:
;;;5028                       pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
00007e  f1010044          ADD      r0,r1,#0x44
000082  f8402024          STR      r2,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
;;;5029                       break;
000086  e00c              B        |L38.162|
;;;5030   
;;;5031                   case eSetValueWithoutOverwrite:
;;;5032   
;;;5033                       if( ucOriginalNotifyState != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000088  f1bc0f02          CMP      r12,#2
00008c  d004              BEQ      |L38.152|
;;;5034                       {
;;;5035                           pxTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToNotify ] = ulValue;
00008e  f1010044          ADD      r0,r1,#0x44
000092  f8402024          STR      r2,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
000096  e001              B        |L38.156|
                  |L38.152|
;;;5036                       }
;;;5037                       else
;;;5038                       {
;;;5039                           /* The value could not be written to the task. */
;;;5040                           xReturn = pdFAIL;
000098  f04f0900          MOV      r9,#0
                  |L38.156|
;;;5041                       }
;;;5042   
;;;5043                       break;
00009c  e001              B        |L38.162|
;;;5044   
;;;5045                   case eNoAction:
;;;5046   
;;;5047                       /* The task is being notified without its notify value being
;;;5048                        * updated. */
;;;5049                       break;
00009e  e000              B        |L38.162|
                  |L38.160|
;;;5050   
;;;5051                   default:
;;;5052   
;;;5053                       /* Should not get here if all enums are handled.
;;;5054                        * Artificially force an assert by testing a value the
;;;5055                        * compiler can't assume is const. */
;;;5056                       configASSERT( xTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0 );
;;;5057                       break;
0000a0  bf00              NOP      
                  |L38.162|
0000a2  bf00              NOP                            ;5021
;;;5058               }
;;;5059   
;;;5060               traceTASK_NOTIFY_FROM_ISR( uxIndexToNotify );
;;;5061   
;;;5062               /* If the task is in the blocked state specifically to wait for a
;;;5063                * notification then unblock it now. */
;;;5064               if( ucOriginalNotifyState == taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION )
0000a4  f1bc0f01          CMP      r12,#1
0000a8  d17e              BNE      |L38.424|
;;;5065               {
;;;5066                   /* The task should not have been on an event list. */
;;;5067                   configASSERT( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) == NULL );
;;;5068   
;;;5069                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
0000aa  484d              LDR      r0,|L38.480|
0000ac  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
0000ae  2800              CMP      r0,#0
0000b0  d15f              BNE      |L38.370|
;;;5070                   {
;;;5071                       listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
0000b2  6948              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x14]
0000b4  e9d1ab02          LDRD     r10,r11,[r1,#8]
0000b8  f8cab008          STR      r11,[r10,#8]
0000bc  e9d1ba02          LDRD     r11,r10,[r1,#8]
0000c0  f8cab004          STR      r11,[r10,#4]
0000c4  f1010a04          ADD      r10,r1,#4
0000c8  f8d0b004          LDR      r11,[r0,#4]
0000cc  45d3              CMP      r11,r10
0000ce  d103              BNE      |L38.216|
0000d0  f8d1a00c          LDR      r10,[r1,#0xc]
0000d4  f8c0a004          STR      r10,[r0,#4]
                  |L38.216|
0000d8  f04f0b00          MOV      r11,#0
0000dc  f8c1b014          STR      r11,[r1,#0x14]
0000e0  f8d0a000          LDR      r10,[r0,#0]
0000e4  f1aa0a01          SUB      r10,r10,#1
0000e8  f8c0a000          STR      r10,[r0,#0]
;;;5072                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
0000ec  f891a02c          LDRB     r10,[r1,#0x2c]
0000f0  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000f2  fa00f00a          LSL      r0,r0,r10
0000f6  f8dfa0ec          LDR      r10,|L38.484|
0000fa  f8daa000          LDR      r10,[r10,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000fe  ea40000a          ORR      r0,r0,r10
000102  f8dfa0e0          LDR      r10,|L38.484|
000106  f8ca0000          STR      r0,[r10,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00010a  f8d1a02c          LDR      r10,[r1,#0x2c]
00010e  eb0a0a8a          ADD      r10,r10,r10,LSL #2
000112  f8dfb0d4          LDR      r11,|L38.488|
000116  eb0b0a8a          ADD      r10,r11,r10,LSL #2
00011a  f8da0004          LDR      r0,[r10,#4]
00011e  6088              STR      r0,[r1,#8]
000120  f8d0b008          LDR      r11,[r0,#8]
000124  f8c1b00c          STR      r11,[r1,#0xc]
000128  f1010a04          ADD      r10,r1,#4
00012c  f8d0b008          LDR      r11,[r0,#8]
000130  f8cba004          STR      r10,[r11,#4]
000134  f8c0a008          STR      r10,[r0,#8]
000138  f8d1a02c          LDR      r10,[r1,#0x2c]
00013c  eb0a0a8a          ADD      r10,r10,r10,LSL #2
000140  f8dfb0a4          LDR      r11,|L38.488|
000144  eb0b0b8a          ADD      r11,r11,r10,LSL #2
000148  f8c1b014          STR      r11,[r1,#0x14]
00014c  f8d1a02c          LDR      r10,[r1,#0x2c]
000150  eb0a0a8a          ADD      r10,r10,r10,LSL #2
000154  f8dfb090          LDR      r11,|L38.488|
000158  f85ba02a          LDR      r10,[r11,r10,LSL #2]
00015c  f10a0a01          ADD      r10,r10,#1
000160  f8d1b02c          LDR      r11,[r1,#0x2c]
000164  eb0b0b8b          ADD      r11,r11,r11,LSL #2
000168  f8dfe07c          LDR      lr,|L38.488|
00016c  f84ea02b          STR      r10,[lr,r11,LSL #2]
000170  e01e              B        |L38.432|
                  |L38.370|
;;;5073                   }
;;;5074                   else
;;;5075                   {
;;;5076                       /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold
;;;5077                        * this task pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;5078                       listINSERT_END( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000172  f8dfa078          LDR      r10,|L38.492|
000176  f8da0004          LDR      r0,[r10,#4]  ; xPendingReadyList
00017a  61c8              STR      r0,[r1,#0x1c]
00017c  f8d0b008          LDR      r11,[r0,#8]
000180  f8c1b020          STR      r11,[r1,#0x20]
000184  f1010a18          ADD      r10,r1,#0x18
000188  f8d0b008          LDR      r11,[r0,#8]
00018c  f8cba004          STR      r10,[r11,#4]
000190  f8c0a008          STR      r10,[r0,#8]
000194  f8dfb054          LDR      r11,|L38.492|
000198  f8c1b028          STR      r11,[r1,#0x28]
00019c  46da              MOV      r10,r11
00019e  f8daa000          LDR      r10,[r10,#0]  ; xPendingReadyList
0001a2  f10a0a01          ADD      r10,r10,#1
0001a6  e000              B        |L38.426|
                  |L38.424|
0001a8  e013              B        |L38.466|
                  |L38.426|
0001aa  f8cba000          STR      r10,[r11,#0]  ; xPendingReadyList
0001ae  bf00              NOP      
                  |L38.432|
;;;5079                   }
;;;5080   
;;;5081                   if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
0001b0  f8dfa03c          LDR      r10,|L38.496|
0001b4  6ac8              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x2c]
0001b6  f8daa000          LDR      r10,[r10,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0001ba  f8daa02c          LDR      r10,[r10,#0x2c]
0001be  4550              CMP      r0,r10
0001c0  d907              BLS      |L38.466|
;;;5082                   {
;;;5083                       /* The notified task has a priority above the currently
;;;5084                        * executing task so a yield is required. */
;;;5085                       if( pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken != NULL )
0001c2  b10f              CBZ      r7,|L38.456|
;;;5086                       {
;;;5087                           *pxHigherPriorityTaskWoken = pdTRUE;
0001c4  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0001c6  6038              STR      r0,[r7,#0]
                  |L38.456|
;;;5088                       }
;;;5089   
;;;5090                       /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;5091                        * using the "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter to an ISR
;;;5092                        * safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;5093                       xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
0001c8  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0001ca  f8dfa028          LDR      r10,|L38.500|
0001ce  f8ca0000          STR      r0,[r10,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L38.466|
;;;5094                   }
;;;5095                   else
;;;5096                   {
;;;5097                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;5098                   }
;;;5099               }
;;;5100           }
;;;5101           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
0001d2  bf00              NOP      
0001d4  f3888811          MSR      BASEPRI,r8
0001d8  bf00              NOP      
;;;5102   
;;;5103           return xReturn;
0001da  4648              MOV      r0,r9
;;;5104       }
0001dc  e8bd8ff0          POP      {r4-r11,pc}
;;;5105   
                          ENDP

                  |L38.480|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L38.484|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L38.488|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L38.492|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L38.496|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L38.500|
                          DCD      xYieldPending

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear PROC
;;;5199   
;;;5200       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyStateClear( TaskHandle_t xTask,
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;5201                                                UBaseType_t uxIndexToClear )
;;;5202       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
000006  460c              MOV      r4,r1
;;;5203           TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;5204           BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;5205   
;;;5206           configASSERT( uxIndexToClear < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;5207   
;;;5208           /* If null is passed in here then it is the calling task that is having
;;;5209            * its notification state cleared. */
;;;5210           pxTCB = prvGetTCBFromHandle( xTask );
000008  b916              CBNZ     r6,|L39.16|
00000a  480c              LDR      r0,|L39.60|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00000e  e000              B        |L39.18|
                  |L39.16|
000010  4630              MOV      r0,r6
                  |L39.18|
000012  4607              MOV      r7,r0
;;;5211   
;;;5212           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000014  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;5213           {
;;;5214               if( pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToClear ] == taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000018  f1070048          ADD      r0,r7,#0x48
00001c  5d00              LDRB     r0,[r0,r4]
00001e  2802              CMP      r0,#2
000020  d105              BNE      |L39.46|
;;;5215               {
;;;5216                   pxTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToClear ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
000022  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000024  f1070048          ADD      r0,r7,#0x48
000028  5501              STRB     r1,[r0,r4]
;;;5217                   xReturn = pdPASS;
00002a  2501              MOVS     r5,#1
00002c  e000              B        |L39.48|
                  |L39.46|
;;;5218               }
;;;5219               else
;;;5220               {
;;;5221                   xReturn = pdFAIL;
00002e  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
                  |L39.48|
;;;5222               }
;;;5223           }
;;;5224           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000030  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;5225   
;;;5226           return xReturn;
000034  4628              MOV      r0,r5
;;;5227       }
000036  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;5228   
                          ENDP

00003a  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L39.60|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGenericNotifyWait||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGenericNotifyWait PROC
;;;4762   
;;;4763       BaseType_t xTaskGenericNotifyWait( UBaseType_t uxIndexToWait,
000000  e92d47f0          PUSH     {r4-r10,lr}
;;;4764                                          uint32_t ulBitsToClearOnEntry,
;;;4765                                          uint32_t ulBitsToClearOnExit,
;;;4766                                          uint32_t * pulNotificationValue,
;;;4767                                          TickType_t xTicksToWait )
;;;4768       {
000004  4604              MOV      r4,r0
000006  460f              MOV      r7,r1
000008  4690              MOV      r8,r2
00000a  461d              MOV      r5,r3
00000c  f8dd9020          LDR      r9,[sp,#0x20]
;;;4769           BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;4770   
;;;4771           configASSERT( uxIndexToWait < configTASK_NOTIFICATION_ARRAY_ENTRIES );
;;;4772   
;;;4773           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000010  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4774           {
;;;4775               /* Only block if a notification is not already pending. */
;;;4776               if( pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
000014  4823              LDR      r0,|L40.164|
000016  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000018  3048              ADDS     r0,r0,#0x48
00001a  5d00              LDRB     r0,[r0,r4]
00001c  2802              CMP      r0,#2
00001e  d01a              BEQ      |L40.86|
;;;4777               {
;;;4778                   /* Clear bits in the task's notification value as bits may get
;;;4779                    * set  by the notifying task or interrupt.  This can be used to
;;;4780                    * clear the value to zero. */
;;;4781                   pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] &= ~ulBitsToClearOnEntry;
000020  4920              LDR      r1,|L40.164|
000022  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000024  3144              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x44
000026  eb010084          ADD      r0,r1,r4,LSL #2
00002a  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
00002c  43b9              BICS     r1,r1,r7
00002e  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;4782   
;;;4783                   /* Mark this task as waiting for a notification. */
;;;4784                   pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] = taskWAITING_NOTIFICATION;
000030  2101              MOVS     r1,#1
000032  481c              LDR      r0,|L40.164|
000034  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000036  3048              ADDS     r0,r0,#0x48
000038  5501              STRB     r1,[r0,r4]
;;;4785   
;;;4786                   if( xTicksToWait > ( TickType_t ) 0 )
00003a  f1b90f00          CMP      r9,#0
00003e  d00a              BEQ      |L40.86|
;;;4787                   {
;;;4788                       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList( xTicksToWait, pdTRUE );
000040  4648              MOV      r0,r9
000042  f7fffffe          BL       prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList
;;;4789                       traceTASK_NOTIFY_WAIT_BLOCK( uxIndexToWait );
;;;4790   
;;;4791                       /* All ports are written to allow a yield in a critical
;;;4792                        * section (some will yield immediately, others wait until the
;;;4793                        * critical section exits) - but it is not something that
;;;4794                        * application code should ever do. */
;;;4795                       portYIELD_WITHIN_API();
000046  f04f5080          MOV      r0,#0x10000000
00004a  4917              LDR      r1,|L40.168|
00004c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
00004e  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
000052  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L40.86|
;;;4796                   }
;;;4797                   else
;;;4798                   {
;;;4799                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4800                   }
;;;4801               }
;;;4802               else
;;;4803               {
;;;4804                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;4805               }
;;;4806           }
;;;4807           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
000056  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;4808   
;;;4809           taskENTER_CRITICAL();
00005a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;4810           {
;;;4811               traceTASK_NOTIFY_WAIT( uxIndexToWait );
;;;4812   
;;;4813               if( pulNotificationValue != NULL )
00005e  b12d              CBZ      r5,|L40.108|
;;;4814               {
;;;4815                   /* Output the current notification value, which may or may not
;;;4816                    * have changed. */
;;;4817                   *pulNotificationValue = pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ];
000060  4810              LDR      r0,|L40.164|
000062  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000064  3044              ADDS     r0,r0,#0x44
000066  f8500024          LDR      r0,[r0,r4,LSL #2]
00006a  6028              STR      r0,[r5,#0]
                  |L40.108|
;;;4818               }
;;;4819   
;;;4820               /* If ucNotifyValue is set then either the task never entered the
;;;4821                * blocked state (because a notification was already pending) or the
;;;4822                * task unblocked because of a notification.  Otherwise the task
;;;4823                * unblocked because of a timeout. */
;;;4824               if( pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] != taskNOTIFICATION_RECEIVED )
00006c  480d              LDR      r0,|L40.164|
00006e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000070  3048              ADDS     r0,r0,#0x48
000072  5d00              LDRB     r0,[r0,r4]
000074  2802              CMP      r0,#2
000076  d001              BEQ      |L40.124|
;;;4825               {
;;;4826                   /* A notification was not received. */
;;;4827                   xReturn = pdFALSE;
000078  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
00007a  e009              B        |L40.144|
                  |L40.124|
;;;4828               }
;;;4829               else
;;;4830               {
;;;4831                   /* A notification was already pending or a notification was
;;;4832                    * received while the task was waiting. */
;;;4833                   pxCurrentTCB->ulNotifiedValue[ uxIndexToWait ] &= ~ulBitsToClearOnExit;
00007c  4909              LDR      r1,|L40.164|
00007e  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000080  3144              ADDS     r1,r1,#0x44
000082  eb010084          ADD      r0,r1,r4,LSL #2
000086  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
000088  ea210108          BIC      r1,r1,r8
00008c  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;4834                   xReturn = pdTRUE;
00008e  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
                  |L40.144|
;;;4835               }
;;;4836   
;;;4837               pxCurrentTCB->ucNotifyState[ uxIndexToWait ] = taskNOT_WAITING_NOTIFICATION;
000090  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
000092  4804              LDR      r0,|L40.164|
000094  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000096  3048              ADDS     r0,r0,#0x48
000098  5501              STRB     r1,[r0,r4]
;;;4838           }
;;;4839           taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00009a  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;4840   
;;;4841           return xReturn;
00009e  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;4842       }
0000a0  e8bd87f0          POP      {r4-r10,pc}
;;;4843   
                          ENDP

                  |L40.164|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L40.168|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle PROC
;;;4021   
;;;4022       TaskHandle_t xTaskGetCurrentTaskHandle( void )
000000  4901              LDR      r1,|L41.8|
;;;4023       {
;;;4024           TaskHandle_t xReturn;
;;;4025   
;;;4026           /* A critical section is not required as this is not called from
;;;4027            * an interrupt and the current TCB will always be the same for any
;;;4028            * individual execution thread. */
;;;4029           xReturn = pxCurrentTCB;
000002  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
;;;4030   
;;;4031           return xReturn;
;;;4032       }
000004  4770              BX       lr
;;;4033   
                          ENDP

000006  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L41.8|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetSchedulerState||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetSchedulerState PROC
;;;4038   
;;;4039       BaseType_t xTaskGetSchedulerState( void )
000000  4905              LDR      r1,|L42.24|
;;;4040       {
;;;4041           BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;4042   
;;;4043           if( xSchedulerRunning == pdFALSE )
000002  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; xSchedulerRunning
000004  b909              CBNZ     r1,|L42.10|
;;;4044           {
;;;4045               xReturn = taskSCHEDULER_NOT_STARTED;
000006  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000008  e005              B        |L42.22|
                  |L42.10|
;;;4046           }
;;;4047           else
;;;4048           {
;;;4049               if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
00000a  4904              LDR      r1,|L42.28|
00000c  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00000e  b909              CBNZ     r1,|L42.20|
;;;4050               {
;;;4051                   xReturn = taskSCHEDULER_RUNNING;
000010  2002              MOVS     r0,#2
000012  e000              B        |L42.22|
                  |L42.20|
;;;4052               }
;;;4053               else
;;;4054               {
;;;4055                   xReturn = taskSCHEDULER_SUSPENDED;
000014  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L42.22|
;;;4056               }
;;;4057           }
;;;4058   
;;;4059           return xReturn;
;;;4060       }
000016  4770              BX       lr
;;;4061   
                          ENDP

                  |L42.24|
                          DCD      xSchedulerRunning
                  |L42.28|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetTickCount||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetTickCount PROC
;;;2285   
;;;2286   TickType_t xTaskGetTickCount( void )
000000  4901              LDR      r1,|L43.8|
;;;2287   {
;;;2288       TickType_t xTicks;
;;;2289   
;;;2290       /* Critical section required if running on a 16 bit processor. */
;;;2291       portTICK_TYPE_ENTER_CRITICAL();
;;;2292       {
;;;2293           xTicks = xTickCount;
000002  6808              LDR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2294       }
;;;2295       portTICK_TYPE_EXIT_CRITICAL();
;;;2296   
;;;2297       return xTicks;
;;;2298   }
000004  4770              BX       lr
;;;2299   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000006  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L43.8|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.xTaskGetTickCountFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskGetTickCountFromISR PROC
;;;2300   
;;;2301   TickType_t xTaskGetTickCountFromISR( void )
000000  2100              MOVS     r1,#0
;;;2302   {
;;;2303       TickType_t xReturn;
;;;2304       UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;2305   
;;;2306       /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a maximum
;;;2307        * system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.  Interrupts that are
;;;2308        * above the maximum system call priority are kept permanently enabled, even
;;;2309        * when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section, but cannot make any calls to
;;;2310        * FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT() is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h
;;;2311        * then portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;2312        * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has been
;;;2313        * assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call priority.
;;;2314        * Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called from interrupts
;;;2315        * that have been assigned a priority at or (logically) below the maximum
;;;2316        * system call  interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a separate interrupt
;;;2317        * safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as simple as possible.
;;;2318        * More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is provided on the following
;;;2319        * link: https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;2320       portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;2321   
;;;2322       uxSavedInterruptStatus = portTICK_TYPE_SET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
;;;2323       {
;;;2324           xReturn = xTickCount;
000002  4a01              LDR      r2,|L44.8|
000004  6810              LDR      r0,[r2,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2325       }
;;;2326       portTICK_TYPE_CLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
;;;2327   
;;;2328       return xReturn;
;;;2329   }
000006  4770              BX       lr
;;;2330   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L44.8|
                          DCD      xTickCount

                          AREA ||i.xTaskIncrementTick||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskIncrementTick PROC
;;;2719   
;;;2720   BaseType_t xTaskIncrementTick( void )
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;2721   {
;;;2722       TCB_t * pxTCB;
;;;2723       TickType_t xItemValue;
;;;2724       BaseType_t xSwitchRequired = pdFALSE;
000004  2700              MOVS     r7,#0
;;;2725   
;;;2726       /* Called by the portable layer each time a tick interrupt occurs.
;;;2727        * Increments the tick then checks to see if the new tick value will cause any
;;;2728        * tasks to be unblocked. */
;;;2729       traceTASK_INCREMENT_TICK( xTickCount );
;;;2730   
;;;2731       if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000006  4857              LDR      r0,|L45.356|
000008  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00000a  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00000c  d17e              BNE      |L45.268|
;;;2732       {
;;;2733           /* Minor optimisation.  The tick count cannot change in this
;;;2734            * block. */
;;;2735           const TickType_t xConstTickCount = xTickCount + ( TickType_t ) 1;
00000e  4856              LDR      r0,|L45.360|
000010  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
000012  1c45              ADDS     r5,r0,#1
;;;2736   
;;;2737           /* Increment the RTOS tick, switching the delayed and overflowed
;;;2738            * delayed lists if it wraps to 0. */
;;;2739           xTickCount = xConstTickCount;
000014  4854              LDR      r0,|L45.360|
000016  6005              STR      r5,[r0,#0]  ; xTickCount
;;;2740   
;;;2741           if( xConstTickCount == ( TickType_t ) 0U ) /*lint !e774 'if' does not always evaluate to false as it is looking for an overflow. */
000018  b98d              CBNZ     r5,|L45.62|
;;;2742           {
;;;2743               taskSWITCH_DELAYED_LISTS();
00001a  4854              LDR      r0,|L45.364|
00001c  f8d08000          LDR      r8,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000020  4853              LDR      r0,|L45.368|
000022  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
000024  4951              LDR      r1,|L45.364|
000026  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000028  4851              LDR      r0,|L45.368|
00002a  f8c08000          STR      r8,[r0,#0]  ; pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
00002e  4851              LDR      r0,|L45.372|
000030  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000032  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
000034  494f              LDR      r1,|L45.372|
000036  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xNumOfOverflows
000038  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
00003c  bf00              NOP      
                  |L45.62|
;;;2744           }
;;;2745           else
;;;2746           {
;;;2747               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2748           }
;;;2749   
;;;2750           /* See if this tick has made a timeout expire.  Tasks are stored in
;;;2751            * the  queue in the order of their wake time - meaning once one task
;;;2752            * has been found whose block time has not expired there is no need to
;;;2753            * look any further down the list. */
;;;2754           if( xConstTickCount >= xNextTaskUnblockTime )
00003e  484e              LDR      r0,|L45.376|
000040  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
000042  4285              CMP      r5,r0
000044  d376              BCC      |L45.308|
;;;2755           {
;;;2756               for( ; ; )
000046  bf00              NOP      
                  |L45.72|
;;;2757               {
;;;2758                   if( listLIST_IS_EMPTY( pxDelayedTaskList ) != pdFALSE )
000048  4848              LDR      r0,|L45.364|
00004a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
00004c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]
00004e  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L45.84|
000050  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000052  e000              B        |L45.86|
                  |L45.84|
000054  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
                  |L45.86|
000056  b120              CBZ      r0,|L45.98|
;;;2759                   {
;;;2760                       /* The delayed list is empty.  Set xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2761                        * to the maximum possible value so it is extremely
;;;2762                        * unlikely that the
;;;2763                        * if( xTickCount >= xNextTaskUnblockTime ) test will pass
;;;2764                        * next time through. */
;;;2765                       xNextTaskUnblockTime = portMAX_DELAY; /*lint !e961 MISRA exception as the casts are only redundant for some ports. */
000058  f04f30ff          MOV      r0,#0xffffffff
00005c  4946              LDR      r1,|L45.376|
00005e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2766                       break;
000060  e067              B        |L45.306|
                  |L45.98|
;;;2767                   }
;;;2768                   else
;;;2769                   {
;;;2770                       /* The delayed list is not empty, get the value of the
;;;2771                        * item at the head of the delayed list.  This is the time
;;;2772                        * at which the task at the head of the delayed list must
;;;2773                        * be removed from the Blocked state. */
;;;2774                       pxTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( pxDelayedTaskList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000062  4842              LDR      r0,|L45.364|
000064  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxDelayedTaskList
000066  68c0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0xc]
000068  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
;;;2775                       xItemValue = listGET_LIST_ITEM_VALUE( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
00006a  6866              LDR      r6,[r4,#4]
;;;2776   
;;;2777                       if( xConstTickCount < xItemValue )
00006c  42b5              CMP      r5,r6
00006e  d202              BCS      |L45.118|
;;;2778                       {
;;;2779                           /* It is not time to unblock this item yet, but the
;;;2780                            * item value is the time at which the task at the head
;;;2781                            * of the blocked list must be removed from the Blocked
;;;2782                            * state -  so record the item value in
;;;2783                            * xNextTaskUnblockTime. */
;;;2784                           xNextTaskUnblockTime = xItemValue;
000070  4841              LDR      r0,|L45.376|
000072  6006              STR      r6,[r0,#0]  ; xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;2785                           break; /*lint !e9011 Code structure here is deemed easier to understand with multiple breaks. */
000074  e05d              B        |L45.306|
                  |L45.118|
;;;2786                       }
;;;2787                       else
;;;2788                       {
;;;2789                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2790                       }
;;;2791   
;;;2792                       /* It is time to remove the item from the Blocked state. */
;;;2793                       listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000076  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
000078  e9d41202          LDRD     r1,r2,[r4,#8]
00007c  608a              STR      r2,[r1,#8]
00007e  e9d42102          LDRD     r2,r1,[r4,#8]
000082  604a              STR      r2,[r1,#4]
000084  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000086  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000088  428a              CMP      r2,r1
00008a  d101              BNE      |L45.144|
00008c  68e1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0xc]
00008e  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
                  |L45.144|
000090  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
000092  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
000094  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
000096  1e49              SUBS     r1,r1,#1
000098  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;2794   
;;;2795                       /* Is the task waiting on an event also?  If so remove
;;;2796                        * it from the event list. */
;;;2797                       if( listLIST_ITEM_CONTAINER( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) ) != NULL )
00009a  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
00009c  b198              CBZ      r0,|L45.198|
;;;2798                       {
;;;2799                           listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00009e  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
0000a0  e9d41207          LDRD     r1,r2,[r4,#0x1c]
0000a4  608a              STR      r2,[r1,#8]
0000a6  e9d42107          LDRD     r2,r1,[r4,#0x1c]
0000aa  604a              STR      r2,[r1,#4]
0000ac  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
0000b0  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
0000b2  428a              CMP      r2,r1
0000b4  d101              BNE      |L45.186|
0000b6  6a21              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x20]
0000b8  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
                  |L45.186|
0000ba  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
0000bc  62a2              STR      r2,[r4,#0x28]
0000be  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
0000c0  1e49              SUBS     r1,r1,#1
0000c2  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
0000c4  bf00              NOP      
                  |L45.198|
;;;2800                       }
;;;2801                       else
;;;2802                       {
;;;2803                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2804                       }
;;;2805   
;;;2806                       /* Place the unblocked task into the appropriate ready
;;;2807                        * list. */
;;;2808                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
0000c6  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000ca  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000cc  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
0000ce  492b              LDR      r1,|L45.380|
0000d0  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000d2  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
0000d4  4929              LDR      r1,|L45.380|
0000d6  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
0000d8  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000da  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000de  4a28              LDR      r2,|L45.384|
0000e0  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000e4  6848              LDR      r0,[r1,#4]
0000e6  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
0000e8  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
0000ea  60e2              STR      r2,[r4,#0xc]
0000ec  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
0000ee  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
0000f0  6051              STR      r1,[r2,#4]
0000f2  6081              STR      r1,[r0,#8]
0000f4  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000f6  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000fa  4a21              LDR      r2,|L45.384|
0000fc  eb020281          ADD      r2,r2,r1,LSL #2
000100  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
000102  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000104  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000108  4a1d              LDR      r2,|L45.384|
00010a  e000              B        |L45.270|
                  |L45.268|
00010c  e022              B        |L45.340|
                  |L45.270|
00010e  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
000112  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
000114  6ae2              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x2c]
000116  eb020282          ADD      r2,r2,r2,LSL #2
00011a  4b19              LDR      r3,|L45.384|
00011c  f8431022          STR      r1,[r3,r2,LSL #2]
;;;2809   
;;;2810                       /* A task being unblocked cannot cause an immediate
;;;2811                        * context switch if preemption is turned off. */
;;;2812                       #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
;;;2813                       {
;;;2814                           /* Preemption is on, but a context switch should
;;;2815                            * only be performed if the unblocked task's
;;;2816                            * priority is higher than the currently executing
;;;2817                            * task.
;;;2818                            * The case of equal priority tasks sharing
;;;2819                            * processing time (which happens when both
;;;2820                            * preemption and time slicing are on) is
;;;2821                            * handled below.*/
;;;2822                           if( pxTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000120  4918              LDR      r1,|L45.388|
000122  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000124  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000126  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
000128  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00012a  d98d              BLS      |L45.72|
;;;2823                           {
;;;2824                               xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
00012c  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
00012e  e78b              B        |L45.72|
000130  e000              B        |L45.308|
                  |L45.306|
000132  bf00              NOP                            ;2766
                  |L45.308|
;;;2825                           }
;;;2826                           else
;;;2827                           {
;;;2828                               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2829                           }
;;;2830                       }
;;;2831                       #endif /* configUSE_PREEMPTION */
;;;2832                   }
;;;2833               }
;;;2834           }
;;;2835   
;;;2836           /* Tasks of equal priority to the currently running task will share
;;;2837            * processing time (time slice) if preemption is on, and the application
;;;2838            * writer has not explicitly turned time slicing off. */
;;;2839           #if ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configUSE_TIME_SLICING == 1 ) )
;;;2840           {
;;;2841               if( listCURRENT_LIST_LENGTH( &( pxReadyTasksLists[ pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority ] ) ) > ( UBaseType_t ) 1 )
000134  4813              LDR      r0,|L45.388|
000136  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
000138  6ac0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0x2c]
00013a  eb000080          ADD      r0,r0,r0,LSL #2
00013e  4910              LDR      r1,|L45.384|
000140  f8510020          LDR      r0,[r1,r0,LSL #2]
000144  2801              CMP      r0,#1
000146  d900              BLS      |L45.330|
;;;2842               {
;;;2843                   xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
000148  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
                  |L45.330|
;;;2844               }
;;;2845               else
;;;2846               {
;;;2847                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2848               }
;;;2849           }
;;;2850           #endif /* ( ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 ) && ( configUSE_TIME_SLICING == 1 ) ) */
;;;2851   
;;;2852           #if ( configUSE_TICK_HOOK == 1 )
;;;2853           {
;;;2854               /* Guard against the tick hook being called when the pended tick
;;;2855                * count is being unwound (when the scheduler is being unlocked). */
;;;2856               if( xPendedTicks == ( TickType_t ) 0 )
;;;2857               {
;;;2858                   vApplicationTickHook();
;;;2859               }
;;;2860               else
;;;2861               {
;;;2862                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2863               }
;;;2864           }
;;;2865           #endif /* configUSE_TICK_HOOK */
;;;2866   
;;;2867           #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION == 1 )
;;;2868           {
;;;2869               if( xYieldPending != pdFALSE )
00014a  480f              LDR      r0,|L45.392|
00014c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xYieldPending
00014e  b100              CBZ      r0,|L45.338|
;;;2870               {
;;;2871                   xSwitchRequired = pdTRUE;
000150  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
                  |L45.338|
;;;2872               }
;;;2873               else
;;;2874               {
;;;2875                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2876               }
;;;2877           }
;;;2878           #endif /* configUSE_PREEMPTION */
;;;2879       }
000152  e004              B        |L45.350|
                  |L45.340|
;;;2880       else
;;;2881       {
;;;2882           ++xPendedTicks;
000154  480d              LDR      r0,|L45.396|
000156  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
000158  1c40              ADDS     r0,r0,#1
00015a  490c              LDR      r1,|L45.396|
00015c  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
                  |L45.350|
;;;2883   
;;;2884           /* The tick hook gets called at regular intervals, even if the
;;;2885            * scheduler is locked. */
;;;2886           #if ( configUSE_TICK_HOOK == 1 )
;;;2887           {
;;;2888               vApplicationTickHook();
;;;2889           }
;;;2890           #endif
;;;2891       }
;;;2892   
;;;2893       return xSwitchRequired;
00015e  4638              MOV      r0,r7
;;;2894   }
000160  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;2895   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L45.356|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L45.360|
                          DCD      xTickCount
                  |L45.364|
                          DCD      pxDelayedTaskList
                  |L45.368|
                          DCD      pxOverflowDelayedTaskList
                  |L45.372|
                          DCD      xNumOfOverflows
                  |L45.376|
                          DCD      xNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L45.380|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L45.384|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L45.388|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L45.392|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L45.396|
                          DCD      xPendedTicks

                          AREA ||i.xTaskRemoveFromEventList||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskRemoveFromEventList PROC
;;;3168   
;;;3169   BaseType_t xTaskRemoveFromEventList( const List_t * const pxEventList )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;3170   {
000002  4603              MOV      r3,r0
;;;3171       TCB_t * pxUnblockedTCB;
;;;3172       BaseType_t xReturn;
;;;3173   
;;;3174       /* THIS FUNCTION MUST BE CALLED FROM A CRITICAL SECTION.  It can also be
;;;3175        * called from a critical section within an ISR. */
;;;3176   
;;;3177       /* The event list is sorted in priority order, so the first in the list can
;;;3178        * be removed as it is known to be the highest priority.  Remove the TCB from
;;;3179        * the delayed list, and add it to the ready list.
;;;3180        *
;;;3181        * If an event is for a queue that is locked then this function will never
;;;3182        * get called - the lock count on the queue will get modified instead.  This
;;;3183        * means exclusive access to the event list is guaranteed here.
;;;3184        *
;;;3185        * This function assumes that a check has already been made to ensure that
;;;3186        * pxEventList is not empty. */
;;;3187       pxUnblockedTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( pxEventList ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000004  68d8              LDR      r0,[r3,#0xc]
000006  68c1              LDR      r1,[r0,#0xc]
;;;3188       configASSERT( pxUnblockedTCB );
;;;3189       listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xEventListItem ) );
000008  6a88              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x28]
00000a  e9d14507          LDRD     r4,r5,[r1,#0x1c]
00000e  60a5              STR      r5,[r4,#8]
000010  e9d15407          LDRD     r5,r4,[r1,#0x1c]
000014  6065              STR      r5,[r4,#4]
000016  f1010418          ADD      r4,r1,#0x18
00001a  6845              LDR      r5,[r0,#4]
00001c  42a5              CMP      r5,r4
00001e  d101              BNE      |L46.36|
000020  6a0c              LDR      r4,[r1,#0x20]
000022  6044              STR      r4,[r0,#4]
                  |L46.36|
000024  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
000026  628d              STR      r5,[r1,#0x28]
000028  6804              LDR      r4,[r0,#0]
00002a  1e64              SUBS     r4,r4,#1
00002c  6004              STR      r4,[r0,#0]
;;;3190   
;;;3191       if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
00002e  4830              LDR      r0,|L46.240|
000030  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000032  2800              CMP      r0,#0
000034  d13d              BNE      |L46.178|
;;;3192       {
;;;3193           listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxUnblockedTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000036  6948              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x14]
000038  e9d14502          LDRD     r4,r5,[r1,#8]
00003c  60a5              STR      r5,[r4,#8]
00003e  e9d15402          LDRD     r5,r4,[r1,#8]
000042  6065              STR      r5,[r4,#4]
000044  1d0c              ADDS     r4,r1,#4
000046  6845              LDR      r5,[r0,#4]
000048  42a5              CMP      r5,r4
00004a  d101              BNE      |L46.80|
00004c  68cc              LDR      r4,[r1,#0xc]
00004e  6044              STR      r4,[r0,#4]
                  |L46.80|
000050  2500              MOVS     r5,#0
000052  614d              STR      r5,[r1,#0x14]
000054  6804              LDR      r4,[r0,#0]
000056  1e64              SUBS     r4,r4,#1
000058  6004              STR      r4,[r0,#0]
;;;3194           prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxUnblockedTCB );
00005a  f891402c          LDRB     r4,[r1,#0x2c]
00005e  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000060  40a0              LSLS     r0,r0,r4
000062  4c24              LDR      r4,|L46.244|
000064  6824              LDR      r4,[r4,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000066  4320              ORRS     r0,r0,r4
000068  4c22              LDR      r4,|L46.244|
00006a  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00006c  6acc              LDR      r4,[r1,#0x2c]
00006e  eb040484          ADD      r4,r4,r4,LSL #2
000072  4d21              LDR      r5,|L46.248|
000074  eb050484          ADD      r4,r5,r4,LSL #2
000078  6860              LDR      r0,[r4,#4]
00007a  6088              STR      r0,[r1,#8]
00007c  6885              LDR      r5,[r0,#8]
00007e  60cd              STR      r5,[r1,#0xc]
000080  1d0c              ADDS     r4,r1,#4
000082  6885              LDR      r5,[r0,#8]
000084  606c              STR      r4,[r5,#4]
000086  6084              STR      r4,[r0,#8]
000088  6acc              LDR      r4,[r1,#0x2c]
00008a  eb040484          ADD      r4,r4,r4,LSL #2
00008e  4d1a              LDR      r5,|L46.248|
000090  eb050584          ADD      r5,r5,r4,LSL #2
000094  614d              STR      r5,[r1,#0x14]
000096  6acc              LDR      r4,[r1,#0x2c]
000098  eb040484          ADD      r4,r4,r4,LSL #2
00009c  4d16              LDR      r5,|L46.248|
00009e  f8554024          LDR      r4,[r5,r4,LSL #2]
0000a2  1c64              ADDS     r4,r4,#1
0000a4  6acd              LDR      r5,[r1,#0x2c]
0000a6  eb050585          ADD      r5,r5,r5,LSL #2
0000aa  4e13              LDR      r6,|L46.248|
0000ac  f8464025          STR      r4,[r6,r5,LSL #2]
0000b0  e010              B        |L46.212|
                  |L46.178|
;;;3195   
;;;3196           #if ( configUSE_TICKLESS_IDLE != 0 )
;;;3197           {
;;;3198               /* If a task is blocked on a kernel object then xNextTaskUnblockTime
;;;3199                * might be set to the blocked task's time out time.  If the task is
;;;3200                * unblocked for a reason other than a timeout xNextTaskUnblockTime is
;;;3201                * normally left unchanged, because it is automatically reset to a new
;;;3202                * value when the tick count equals xNextTaskUnblockTime.  However if
;;;3203                * tickless idling is used it might be more important to enter sleep mode
;;;3204                * at the earliest possible time - so reset xNextTaskUnblockTime here to
;;;3205                * ensure it is updated at the earliest possible time. */
;;;3206               prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
;;;3207           }
;;;3208           #endif
;;;3209       }
;;;3210       else
;;;3211       {
;;;3212           /* The delayed and ready lists cannot be accessed, so hold this task
;;;3213            * pending until the scheduler is resumed. */
;;;3214           listINSERT_END( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxUnblockedTCB->xEventListItem ) );
0000b2  4c12              LDR      r4,|L46.252|
0000b4  6860              LDR      r0,[r4,#4]  ; xPendingReadyList
0000b6  61c8              STR      r0,[r1,#0x1c]
0000b8  6885              LDR      r5,[r0,#8]
0000ba  620d              STR      r5,[r1,#0x20]
0000bc  f1010418          ADD      r4,r1,#0x18
0000c0  6885              LDR      r5,[r0,#8]
0000c2  606c              STR      r4,[r5,#4]
0000c4  6084              STR      r4,[r0,#8]
0000c6  4d0d              LDR      r5,|L46.252|
0000c8  628d              STR      r5,[r1,#0x28]
0000ca  462c              MOV      r4,r5
0000cc  6824              LDR      r4,[r4,#0]  ; xPendingReadyList
0000ce  1c64              ADDS     r4,r4,#1
0000d0  602c              STR      r4,[r5,#0]  ; xPendingReadyList
0000d2  bf00              NOP      
                  |L46.212|
;;;3215       }
;;;3216   
;;;3217       if( pxUnblockedTCB->uxPriority > pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
0000d4  4c0a              LDR      r4,|L46.256|
0000d6  6ac8              LDR      r0,[r1,#0x2c]
0000d8  6824              LDR      r4,[r4,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000da  6ae4              LDR      r4,[r4,#0x2c]
0000dc  42a0              CMP      r0,r4
0000de  d904              BLS      |L46.234|
;;;3218       {
;;;3219           /* Return true if the task removed from the event list has a higher
;;;3220            * priority than the calling task.  This allows the calling task to know if
;;;3221            * it should force a context switch now. */
;;;3222           xReturn = pdTRUE;
0000e0  2201              MOVS     r2,#1
;;;3223   
;;;3224           /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not using the
;;;3225            * "xHigherPriorityTaskWoken" parameter to an ISR safe FreeRTOS function. */
;;;3226           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
0000e2  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000e4  4c07              LDR      r4,|L46.260|
0000e6  6020              STR      r0,[r4,#0]  ; xYieldPending
0000e8  e000              B        |L46.236|
                  |L46.234|
;;;3227       }
;;;3228       else
;;;3229       {
;;;3230           xReturn = pdFALSE;
0000ea  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
                  |L46.236|
;;;3231       }
;;;3232   
;;;3233       return xReturn;
0000ec  4610              MOV      r0,r2
;;;3234   }
0000ee  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;3235   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

                  |L46.240|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L46.244|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L46.248|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L46.252|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L46.256|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L46.260|
                          DCD      xYieldPending

                          AREA ||i.xTaskResumeAll||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskResumeAll PROC
;;;2173   
;;;2174   BaseType_t xTaskResumeAll( void )
000000  b570              PUSH     {r4-r6,lr}
;;;2175   {
;;;2176       TCB_t * pxTCB = NULL;
000002  2400              MOVS     r4,#0
;;;2177       BaseType_t xAlreadyYielded = pdFALSE;
000004  2600              MOVS     r6,#0
;;;2178   
;;;2179       /* If uxSchedulerSuspended is zero then this function does not match a
;;;2180        * previous call to vTaskSuspendAll(). */
;;;2181       configASSERT( uxSchedulerSuspended );
;;;2182   
;;;2183       /* It is possible that an ISR caused a task to be removed from an event
;;;2184        * list while the scheduler was suspended.  If this was the case then the
;;;2185        * removed task will have been added to the xPendingReadyList.  Once the
;;;2186        * scheduler has been resumed it is safe to move all the pending ready
;;;2187        * tasks from this list into their appropriate ready list. */
;;;2188       taskENTER_CRITICAL();
000006  f7fffffe          BL       vPortEnterCritical
;;;2189       {
;;;2190           --uxSchedulerSuspended;
00000a  484b              LDR      r0,|L47.312|
00000c  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
00000e  1e40              SUBS     r0,r0,#1
000010  4949              LDR      r1,|L47.312|
000012  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
;;;2191   
;;;2192           if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
000014  4608              MOV      r0,r1
000016  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000018  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00001a  d17d              BNE      |L47.280|
;;;2193           {
;;;2194               if( uxCurrentNumberOfTasks > ( UBaseType_t ) 0U )
00001c  4847              LDR      r0,|L47.316|
00001e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
000020  2800              CMP      r0,#0
000022  d079              BEQ      |L47.280|
;;;2195               {
;;;2196                   /* Move any readied tasks from the pending list into the
;;;2197                    * appropriate ready list. */
;;;2198                   while( listLIST_IS_EMPTY( &xPendingReadyList ) == pdFALSE )
000024  e05b              B        |L47.222|
                  |L47.38|
;;;2199                   {
;;;2200                       pxTCB = listGET_OWNER_OF_HEAD_ENTRY( ( &xPendingReadyList ) ); /*lint !e9079 void * is used as this macro is used with timers and co-routines too.  Alignment is known to be fine as the type of the pointer stored and retrieved is the same. */
000026  4846              LDR      r0,|L47.320|
000028  68c0              LDR      r0,[r0,#0xc]
00002a  68c4              LDR      r4,[r0,#0xc]
;;;2201                       listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
00002c  6aa0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x28]
00002e  e9d41207          LDRD     r1,r2,[r4,#0x1c]
000032  608a              STR      r2,[r1,#8]
000034  e9d42107          LDRD     r2,r1,[r4,#0x1c]
000038  604a              STR      r2,[r1,#4]
00003a  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
00003e  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000040  428a              CMP      r2,r1
000042  d101              BNE      |L47.72|
000044  6a21              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x20]
000046  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
                  |L47.72|
000048  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
00004a  62a2              STR      r2,[r4,#0x28]
00004c  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
00004e  1e49              SUBS     r1,r1,#1
000050  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;2202                       portMEMORY_BARRIER();
;;;2203                       listREMOVE_ITEM( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000052  6960              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x14]
000054  e9d41202          LDRD     r1,r2,[r4,#8]
000058  608a              STR      r2,[r1,#8]
00005a  e9d42102          LDRD     r2,r1,[r4,#8]
00005e  604a              STR      r2,[r1,#4]
000060  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000062  6842              LDR      r2,[r0,#4]
000064  428a              CMP      r2,r1
000066  d101              BNE      |L47.108|
000068  68e1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0xc]
00006a  6041              STR      r1,[r0,#4]
                  |L47.108|
00006c  2200              MOVS     r2,#0
00006e  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
000070  6801              LDR      r1,[r0,#0]
000072  1e49              SUBS     r1,r1,#1
000074  6001              STR      r1,[r0,#0]
;;;2204                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
000076  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00007a  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
00007c  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
00007e  4931              LDR      r1,|L47.324|
000080  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000082  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
000084  492f              LDR      r1,|L47.324|
000086  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000088  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00008a  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
00008e  4a2e              LDR      r2,|L47.328|
000090  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
000094  6848              LDR      r0,[r1,#4]
000096  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
000098  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
00009a  60e2              STR      r2,[r4,#0xc]
00009c  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
00009e  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
0000a0  6051              STR      r1,[r2,#4]
0000a2  6081              STR      r1,[r0,#8]
0000a4  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000a6  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000aa  4a27              LDR      r2,|L47.328|
0000ac  eb020281          ADD      r2,r2,r1,LSL #2
0000b0  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
0000b2  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
0000b4  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
0000b8  4a23              LDR      r2,|L47.328|
0000ba  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
0000be  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
0000c0  6ae2              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x2c]
0000c2  eb020282          ADD      r2,r2,r2,LSL #2
0000c6  4b20              LDR      r3,|L47.328|
0000c8  f8431022          STR      r1,[r3,r2,LSL #2]
;;;2205   
;;;2206                       /* If the moved task has a priority higher than or equal to
;;;2207                        * the current task then a yield must be performed. */
;;;2208                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
0000cc  491f              LDR      r1,|L47.332|
0000ce  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
0000d0  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
0000d2  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
0000d4  4288              CMP      r0,r1
0000d6  d302              BCC      |L47.222|
;;;2209                       {
;;;2210                           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
0000d8  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
0000da  491d              LDR      r1,|L47.336|
0000dc  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L47.222|
0000de  4818              LDR      r0,|L47.320|
0000e0  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]            ;2198  ; xPendingReadyList
0000e2  b908              CBNZ     r0,|L47.232|
0000e4  2001              MOVS     r0,#1                 ;2198
0000e6  e000              B        |L47.234|
                  |L47.232|
0000e8  2000              MOVS     r0,#0                 ;2198
                  |L47.234|
0000ea  2800              CMP      r0,#0                 ;2198
0000ec  d09b              BEQ      |L47.38|
;;;2211                       }
;;;2212                       else
;;;2213                       {
;;;2214                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2215                       }
;;;2216                   }
;;;2217   
;;;2218                   if( pxTCB != NULL )
0000ee  b10c              CBZ      r4,|L47.244|
;;;2219                   {
;;;2220                       /* A task was unblocked while the scheduler was suspended,
