;;;2221                        * which may have prevented the next unblock time from being
;;;2222                        * re-calculated, in which case re-calculate it now.  Mainly
;;;2223                        * important for low power tickless implementations, where
;;;2224                        * this can prevent an unnecessary exit from low power
;;;2225                        * state. */
;;;2226                       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime();
0000f0  f7fffffe          BL       prvResetNextTaskUnblockTime
                  |L47.244|
;;;2227                   }
;;;2228   
;;;2229                   /* If any ticks occurred while the scheduler was suspended then
;;;2230                    * they should be processed now.  This ensures the tick count does
;;;2231                    * not  slip, and that any delayed tasks are resumed at the correct
;;;2232                    * time. */
;;;2233                   {
;;;2234                       TickType_t xPendedCounts = xPendedTicks; /* Non-volatile copy. */
0000f4  4817              LDR      r0,|L47.340|
0000f6  6805              LDR      r5,[r0,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
;;;2235   
;;;2236                       if( xPendedCounts > ( TickType_t ) 0U )
0000f8  b165              CBZ      r5,|L47.276|
;;;2237                       {
;;;2238                           do
0000fa  bf00              NOP      
                  |L47.252|
;;;2239                           {
;;;2240                               if( xTaskIncrementTick() != pdFALSE )
0000fc  f7fffffe          BL       xTaskIncrementTick
000100  b110              CBZ      r0,|L47.264|
;;;2241                               {
;;;2242                                   xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000102  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000104  4912              LDR      r1,|L47.336|
000106  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L47.264|
;;;2243                               }
;;;2244                               else
;;;2245                               {
;;;2246                                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2247                               }
;;;2248   
;;;2249                               --xPendedCounts;
000108  1e6d              SUBS     r5,r5,#1
;;;2250                           } while( xPendedCounts > ( TickType_t ) 0U );
00010a  2d00              CMP      r5,#0
00010c  d1f6              BNE      |L47.252|
;;;2251   
;;;2252                           xPendedTicks = 0;
00010e  2000              MOVS     r0,#0
000110  4910              LDR      r1,|L47.340|
000112  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xPendedTicks
                  |L47.276|
;;;2253                       }
;;;2254                       else
;;;2255                       {
;;;2256                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2257                       }
;;;2258                   }
;;;2259   
;;;2260                   if( xYieldPending != pdFALSE )
000114  480e              LDR      r0,|L47.336|
000116  e000              B        |L47.282|
                  |L47.280|
000118  e009              B        |L47.302|
                  |L47.282|
00011a  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; xYieldPending
00011c  b138              CBZ      r0,|L47.302|
;;;2261                   {
;;;2262                       #if ( configUSE_PREEMPTION != 0 )
;;;2263                       {
;;;2264                           xAlreadyYielded = pdTRUE;
00011e  2601              MOVS     r6,#1
;;;2265                       }
;;;2266                       #endif
;;;2267                       taskYIELD_IF_USING_PREEMPTION();
000120  0730              LSLS     r0,r6,#28
000122  490d              LDR      r1,|L47.344|
000124  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]
000126  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00012a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
                  |L47.302|
;;;2268                   }
;;;2269                   else
;;;2270                   {
;;;2271                       mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2272                   }
;;;2273               }
;;;2274           }
;;;2275           else
;;;2276           {
;;;2277               mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;2278           }
;;;2279       }
;;;2280       taskEXIT_CRITICAL();
00012e  f7fffffe          BL       vPortExitCritical
;;;2281   
;;;2282       return xAlreadyYielded;
000132  4630              MOV      r0,r6
;;;2283   }
000134  bd70              POP      {r4-r6,pc}
;;;2284   /*-----------------------------------------------------------*/
                          ENDP

000136  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L47.312|
                          DCD      uxSchedulerSuspended
                  |L47.316|
                          DCD      uxCurrentNumberOfTasks
                  |L47.320|
                          DCD      xPendingReadyList
                  |L47.324|
                          DCD      uxTopReadyPriority
                  |L47.328|
                          DCD      pxReadyTasksLists
                  |L47.332|
                          DCD      pxCurrentTCB
                  |L47.336|
                          DCD      xYieldPending
                  |L47.340|
                          DCD      xPendedTicks
                  |L47.344|
                          DCD      0xe000ed04

                          AREA ||i.xTaskResumeFromISR||, CODE, READONLY, ALIGN=2

                  xTaskResumeFromISR PROC
;;;1878   
;;;1879       BaseType_t xTaskResumeFromISR( TaskHandle_t xTaskToResume )
000000  e92d41f0          PUSH     {r4-r8,lr}
;;;1880       {
000004  4606              MOV      r6,r0
;;;1881           BaseType_t xYieldRequired = pdFALSE;
000006  2700              MOVS     r7,#0
;;;1882           TCB_t * const pxTCB = xTaskToResume;
000008  4634              MOV      r4,r6
;;;1883           UBaseType_t uxSavedInterruptStatus;
;;;1884   
;;;1885           configASSERT( xTaskToResume );
;;;1886   
;;;1887           /* RTOS ports that support interrupt nesting have the concept of a
;;;1888            * maximum  system call (or maximum API call) interrupt priority.
;;;1889            * Interrupts that are  above the maximum system call priority are keep
;;;1890            * permanently enabled, even when the RTOS kernel is in a critical section,
;;;1891            * but cannot make any calls to FreeRTOS API functions.  If configASSERT()
;;;1892            * is defined in FreeRTOSConfig.h then
;;;1893            * portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID() will result in an assertion
;;;1894            * failure if a FreeRTOS API function is called from an interrupt that has
;;;1895            * been assigned a priority above the configured maximum system call
;;;1896            * priority.  Only FreeRTOS functions that end in FromISR can be called
;;;1897            * from interrupts  that have been assigned a priority at or (logically)
;;;1898            * below the maximum system call interrupt priority.  FreeRTOS maintains a
;;;1899            * separate interrupt safe API to ensure interrupt entry is as fast and as
;;;1900            * simple as possible.  More information (albeit Cortex-M specific) is
;;;1901            * provided on the following link:
;;;1902            * https://www.FreeRTOS.org/RTOS-Cortex-M3-M4.html */
;;;1903           portASSERT_IF_INTERRUPT_PRIORITY_INVALID();
;;;1904   
;;;1905           uxSavedInterruptStatus = portSET_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR();
00000a  bf00              NOP      
00000c  21bf              MOVS     r1,#0xbf
00000e  f3ef8011          MRS      r0,BASEPRI
000012  f3818811          MSR      BASEPRI,r1
000016  f3bf8f4f          DSB      
00001a  f3bf8f6f          ISB      
00001e  bf00              NOP      
000020  4605              MOV      r5,r0
;;;1906           {
;;;1907               if( prvTaskIsTaskSuspended( pxTCB ) != pdFALSE )
000022  4620              MOV      r0,r4
000024  f7fffffe          BL       prvTaskIsTaskSuspended
000028  2800              CMP      r0,#0
00002a  d041              BEQ      |L48.176|
;;;1908               {
;;;1909                   traceTASK_RESUME_FROM_ISR( pxTCB );
;;;1910   
;;;1911                   /* Check the ready lists can be accessed. */
;;;1912                   if( uxSchedulerSuspended == ( UBaseType_t ) pdFALSE )
00002c  4824              LDR      r0,|L48.192|
00002e  6800              LDR      r0,[r0,#0]  ; uxSchedulerSuspended
000030  2800              CMP      r0,#0
000032  d138              BNE      |L48.166|
;;;1913                   {
;;;1914                       /* Ready lists can be accessed so move the task from the
;;;1915                        * suspended list to the ready list directly. */
;;;1916                       if( pxTCB->uxPriority >= pxCurrentTCB->uxPriority )
000034  4923              LDR      r1,|L48.196|
000036  6ae0              LDR      r0,[r4,#0x2c]
000038  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; pxCurrentTCB
00003a  6ac9              LDR      r1,[r1,#0x2c]
00003c  4288              CMP      r0,r1
00003e  d303              BCC      |L48.72|
;;;1917                       {
;;;1918                           xYieldRequired = pdTRUE;
000040  2701              MOVS     r7,#1
;;;1919   
;;;1920                           /* Mark that a yield is pending in case the user is not
;;;1921                            * using the return value to initiate a context switch
;;;1922                            * from the ISR using portYIELD_FROM_ISR. */
;;;1923                           xYieldPending = pdTRUE;
000042  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000044  4920              LDR      r1,|L48.200|
000046  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; xYieldPending
                  |L48.72|
;;;1924                       }
;;;1925                       else
;;;1926                       {
;;;1927                           mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1928                       }
;;;1929   
;;;1930                       ( void ) uxListRemove( &( pxTCB->xStateListItem ) );
000048  1d20              ADDS     r0,r4,#4
00004a  f7fffffe          BL       uxListRemove
;;;1931                       prvAddTaskToReadyList( pxTCB );
00004e  f894102c          LDRB     r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000052  2001              MOVS     r0,#1
000054  4088              LSLS     r0,r0,r1
000056  491d              LDR      r1,|L48.204|
000058  6809              LDR      r1,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
00005a  4308              ORRS     r0,r0,r1
00005c  491b              LDR      r1,|L48.204|
00005e  6008              STR      r0,[r1,#0]  ; uxTopReadyPriority
000060  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
000062  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000066  4a1a              LDR      r2,|L48.208|
000068  eb020181          ADD      r1,r2,r1,LSL #2
00006c  6848              LDR      r0,[r1,#4]
00006e  60a0              STR      r0,[r4,#8]
000070  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
000072  60e2              STR      r2,[r4,#0xc]
000074  1d21              ADDS     r1,r4,#4
000076  6882              LDR      r2,[r0,#8]
000078  6051              STR      r1,[r2,#4]
00007a  6081              STR      r1,[r0,#8]
00007c  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00007e  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000082  4a13              LDR      r2,|L48.208|
000084  eb020281          ADD      r2,r2,r1,LSL #2
000088  6162              STR      r2,[r4,#0x14]
00008a  6ae1              LDR      r1,[r4,#0x2c]
00008c  eb010181          ADD      r1,r1,r1,LSL #2
000090  4a0f              LDR      r2,|L48.208|
000092  f8521021          LDR      r1,[r2,r1,LSL #2]
000096  1c49              ADDS     r1,r1,#1
000098  6ae2              LDR      r2,[r4,#0x2c]
00009a  eb020282          ADD      r2,r2,r2,LSL #2
00009e  4b0c              LDR      r3,|L48.208|
0000a0  f8431022          STR      r1,[r3,r2,LSL #2]
0000a4  e004              B        |L48.176|
                  |L48.166|
;;;1932                   }
;;;1933                   else
;;;1934                   {
;;;1935                       /* The delayed or ready lists cannot be accessed so the task
;;;1936                        * is held in the pending ready list until the scheduler is
;;;1937                        * unsuspended. */
;;;1938                       vListInsertEnd( &( xPendingReadyList ), &( pxTCB->xEventListItem ) );
0000a6  f1040118          ADD      r1,r4,#0x18
0000aa  480a              LDR      r0,|L48.212|
0000ac  f7fffffe          BL       vListInsertEnd
                  |L48.176|
;;;1939                   }
;;;1940               }
;;;1941               else
;;;1942               {
;;;1943                   mtCOVERAGE_TEST_MARKER();
;;;1944               }
;;;1945           }
;;;1946           portCLEAR_INTERRUPT_MASK_FROM_ISR( uxSavedInterruptStatus );
0000b0  bf00              NOP      
0000b2  f3858811          MSR      BASEPRI,r5
0000b6  bf00              NOP      
;;;1947   
;;;1948           return xYieldRequired;
0000b8  4638              MOV      r0,r7
;;;1949       }
0000ba  e8bd81f0          POP      {r4-r8,pc}
;;;1950   
                          ENDP

0000be  0000              DCW      0x0000
                  |L48.192|
